ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002044 (hamburger) is the closest defensible class. No specific FoodOn entry exists for a prawn/ebi burger variant with spicy tomato sauce. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Burger (hamburger type) — bread-based dish with filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers burger-type bread-based dishes. No specific code exists for ebi/prawn burger with spicy tomato sauce. Closest parent match documented.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific fast-food prawn burger preparation. Null used to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger weighs approximately 200 g, providing around 526 kcal, 26.6 g protein, 20.4 g fat, and 59.1 g carbohydrate per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger (cr_id G00010107). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and vitamin/mineral fields not reported by HPB are set to -1 where unavailable.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun / white wheat bread burger — analogous GI estimate based on refined wheat flour bun component
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being a refined white wheat flour burger bun, which the University of Sydney GI Database records at GI ~70–72 for white bread/burger bun equivalents. The deep-fried prawn patties and sauce contribute negligible additional carbohydrate that would materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × available carbohydrate per serving (59.1 g) / 100 = 41.4 GL units (200 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content at 487 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (197 mg/100 g), yielding an unfavourable sodium-to-potassium ratio; a 200 g serving delivers approximately 974 mg sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this burger, as a single serving provides approximately 974 mg sodium — nearly 43% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — with insufficient potassium or dietary fibre to offset the pressor effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat bun (GI ~70) with 29.6 g carbohydrate/100 g and only 1.8 g dietary fibre/100 g, producing a high glycemic load of approximately 41 GL units per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this burger due to its high glycemic load (~41 per serving), low fibre content, and added sugars from the spicy tomato sauce, all of which promote rapid postprandial glucose elevation and may worsen glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of high sodium (487 mg/100 g), cholesterol (29 mg/100 g), deep-fried preparation generating oxidised lipids and AGEs, and ultra-processed food classification with likely saturated and trans fat content.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this burger; the deep-fried double prawn patties, high sodium load, and ultra-processed composition collectively elevate risk of endothelial dysfunction, blood pressure elevation, and atherogenic lipid changes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); spicy tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine; likely trans fat presence; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this burger; deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory AGEs, the tomato-based sauce is a nightshade that may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals, and the overall preparation provides no anti-inflammatory offset.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger is a Singapore fast-food burger featuring two deep-fried prawn (ebi) patties served in a refined white wheat flour bun, topped with spicy tomato sauce, lettuce, and mayonnaise. 'Ebi' is the Japanese word for prawn or shrimp, and ebi burgers are a popular fast-food format in Singapore and Southeast Asia, adapted from Japanese fast-food chains. The double-patty format doubles the prawn content relative to a standard ebi burger, increasing both protein and sodium. The burger has a crispy exterior from deep-frying, a soft bun, and a tangy-spicy flavour profile from the tomato sauce. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, 29.57 g carbohydrate, 5.95 g sugar, 1.8 g dietary fibre, and 487 mg sodium. A typical 200 g serving delivers approximately 526 kcal and 974 mg sodium. The high sodium content, high glycemic index (~70 from the refined bun), low dietary fibre, and deep-fried preparation make this burger clinically unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is classified as an ultra-processed bread-based dish in the grains and staples food group. Allergens include gluten, crustaceans, egg, and soy. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner item in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010107, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The food identity is exact. Saturated fat, fatty acid fractions, vitamins, and water content are not reported by HPB and are set to -1 with documentation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on the refined wheat bun component.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (G00010107) as of April 2026. No alternative approved source provides these values for this specific Singapore fast-food item. 2. The input health_suitability field referenced trans fat at 0.1 g/100 g; however, this value is not confirmed in the HPB source data and trans_fat_g has been set to -1 pending verified source confirmation. 3. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on refined white wheat burger bun data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. 4. Halal status assumed based on typical Singapore fast-food chain compliance; verify against current restaurant certification. 5. Serving size of 200 g is an estimate for a double-patty burger; actual product weight should be verified against current menu specifications.
